title: Auditory verbal hallucinations: The ins and outs of subtypes.

assets.abstract: BACKGROUND: Voice-hearing in psychosis is a heterogeneous and often distressing experience. Phenomenological studies have proposed the existence of several subtypes of auditory verbal hallucinations (AVHs) that may guide intervention, such as inner-speech based voices (ISVs), hypervigilance voices (HVs) and memory-based voices, but empirical data is scarce. We developed a detailed coding scheme with the aim of examining whether distinct subtypes of AVHs could be reliably identified and character

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.
